full_prompt: Design a topology-optimized folding stem and steering column for a commuter e-scooter as an open-source micromobility lightweighting benchmark. The stem replaces a baseline 1.8 kg machined 6061-T6 aluminum lock-and-clamp stem on a commuter e-scooter with 110 kg rider + 25 kg scooter mass. The stem is a monolithic part 600 mm tall with a 35 mm diameter steering column OD, a fold pivot at 200 mm up from the deck with a 25 mm pin hole, and a handlebar clamp at the top for a 22.2 mm OD handlebar. Material is 7075-T6 aluminum (yield 503 MPa, E 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). The stem must survive four independent load cases from EN 17128 e-scooter structural: (LC1) vertical rider 1100 N on handlebar ends, (LC2) 500 N lateral on handlebar ends (one-handed dismount scenario), (LC3) 800 N forward braking load at handlebar grip, (LC4) an impact at handlebar grip from a 1 m fall over (equivalent quasi-static 1500 N). Pass/fail criteria: (R1) max von Mises stress under any of LC1-LC4 must not exceed 335 MPa (7075-T6 yield / SF 1.5), (R2) max handlebar-tip displacement under any static load case must not exceed 10 mm, (R3) stem mass must be at least 30% lower than the 1.8 kg baseline, so <= 1.26 kg, (R4) fold-pivot pin-hole bearing stress must not exceed 600 MPa under LC3, and (R5) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC4 must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body plus a verification report listing max von Mises per load case, handlebar-tip displacement per load case, stem mass, pin-hole bearing stress, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Topology-optimize the folding stem and steering column of a commuter e-scooter (110 kg rider + 25 kg scooter), replacing a 1.8 kg machined 6061-T6 baseline. The redesigned monolithic stem is in 7075-T6 aluminum, roughly 600 mm tall with a 35 mm steering-column OD, a 25 mm fold-pivot pin hole at 200 mm above the deck, and a handlebar clamp at the top for a 22.2 mm handlebar. The stem base is clamped to the front column; the handlebar clamp is the load-application region.

Load cases per EN 17128 (e-scooter structural).
- LC1 rider vertical: 1100 N on the handlebar ends.
- LC2 lateral: 500 N on the handlebar ends (one-handed dismount).
- LC3 forward braking: 800 N at the handlebar grip.
- LC4 fall-over impact at the grip (equivalent quasi-static 1500 N).

Mass target is at least 30% lighter than the 1.8 kg baseline; also check pin-hole bearing stress under LC3 and buckling margin under LC4.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 30% mass reduction target with fold-pivot geometry is delivered by topology optimization around structural FEA.

prompt.title: Folding e-scooter stem redesign (lightweighting benchmark)
prompt.brief: Redesign a 1.8 kg 6061-T6 e-scooter folding stem in 7075-T6 (600 mm tall, 35 mm column OD, fold pivot at 200 mm) to pass EN 17128 load cases with <=1.26 kg and 2x buckling margin.
